meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stuck with no project ideas? Learn how to implement SaaS pricing models to build professional apps. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Build a SaaS pricing module to master backend logic and user psychology. By integrating tiered billing structures like Freemium or Usage-based models, you create a functional, high-value asset for your portfolio that proves you understand the real-world mechanics of building profitable software applications.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking with rhythmic indifference. You have the technical skills, but the 'what' remains a phantom, leaving you paralyzed in a cycle of tutorial hell. The gap between a hobbyist script and a professional SaaS isn't the code—it's the business logic that transforms a project into a product.

Today, we break that cycle. We are going to build a production-ready SaaS pricing engine. This isn't just a UI exercise; it is a masterclass in architecture that will force you to think like a founder. By the end of this, you will have a tangible, deployable feature that separates your portfolio from the thousands of generic To-Do apps cluttering GitHub.

steps.title: Architecting the Database Schema
steps.description: Define the relationship between User, Subscription, and Plan. This ensures your app can handle multi-tenant data structures correctly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS. Define models for User, Plan (Free, Pro, Enterprise), and Subscription with field types for status, stripe_customer_id, and current_period_end.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add schema.prisma --model Subscription --fields 'plan:Plan, status:String, customerId:String'

steps.title: Implementing the Pricing Logic
steps.description: Build the server-side logic that evaluates which features a user can access based on their current subscription tier.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript middleware function that checks a user's subscription status from the database and returns a boolean for feature access permissions.

steps.title: Frontend Pricing Component
steps.description: Create a high-converting pricing table that dynamically updates based on the active plan state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React pricing table component using Tailwind CSS. Include three cards, a toggle for monthly/yearly billing, and hover effects for the CTA buttons.

steps.title: Stripe Integration Flow
steps.description: Connect your pricing UI to a real payment gateway to handle checkout sessions securely.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Next.js API route that handles Stripe checkout session creation, mapping the selected plan ID to the price object.

steps.title: Testing Edge Cases
steps.description: Simulate subscription cancellations and failed payments to ensure your app handles state changes gracefully.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of integration tests to verify that a user's access is revoked immediately when their subscription status updates to 'canceled'.

pro_tips: Always store price amounts in cents (integers) in your database to avoid floating-point math errors common in currency calculations.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your middleware so users aren't locked out exactly at 12:00 AM on renewal day.
Use feature flags instead of hardcoding plan restrictions; this allows you to toggle beta features for Enterprise users without changing core code.
Always log your webhook events from Stripe to a local database table before processing them, providing a perfect audit trail for debugging.
Design your pricing page with a 'Recommended' badge on the middle tier; psychological anchoring significantly boosts conversion rates for your portfolio demo.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is live, deploy it to Vercel and add a 'Manage Subscription' link to the user profile. This completes the full loop of the SaaS lifecycle.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the architecture to build any SaaS imaginable. Stop dreaming about the next big thing and start engineering it. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
